mid_market_rate.note: All four sources cluster tightly around 9.15 GBP/CNY. BoE rate is for previous business day (2026-04-01). XE rate is live.

executive_summary: UK banks charge between 2.2% and 4.6% in exchange rate markup on GBP-to-CNY transfers, with additional flat fees of GBP 5-25 and unpredictable SWIFT intermediary deductions. On a GBP 10,000 payment to a Chinese supplier, the estimated total cost via a major UK bank ranges from approximately GBP 255 (Nationwide) to GBP 410 (NatWest). The fintech landscape offers significantly lower costs: WorldFirst (GBP 54 at 0.5% max FX ceiling), Airwallex (GBP 50 at 0.5% FX, business only with eligibility restrictions), Wise (GBP 91 at 0.91% effective cost via Alipay), and XE (GBP 105 estimated at ~1% opaque markup). Wise's '0% FX markup' model charges a transparent percentage fee that is economically equivalent to a 0.91% markup on GBP 10k — the distinction is transparency of presentation, not economics. The fintech market splits clearly: Airwallex and WorldFirst compete head-to-head for B2B supplier payments with local CNY rails and same-day delivery; Wise, Remitly, and WorldRemit serve personal remittances with CNY caps; OFX is business-only; XE serves both. No UK high-street bank holds CNY or offers same-day arrival. The FX markup represents 85-95% of the true cost of a bank transfer.

providers.fx_markup_notes: Tiered by transfer amount — better rates for larger transfers. Markup of 3.7% was the highest among major UK banks in Wise's 2024 transparency study. GBP-CNY likely at or above average due to exotic currency premium. HSBC does not publish the markup percentage.
providers.transfer_fee_notes: GBP 5 for non-EEA transfers. Free for Premier, Private Banking, and Global Money Account holders. GBP 17 by post.
providers.intermediary_notes: HSBC states 'you may not be able to see what these fees are in advance'. SHA pricing default. OUR available. Airwallex cites correspondent bank charges up to USD 90. Cannot send CNY directly due to PRC regulations — must send GBP/USD/EUR.
providers.account_cost_notes: Personal current account free. Business: GBP 8/month maintenance + GBP 6/month internet banking (after 12-month free startup). Global Money Account free for all current account holders.
providers.speed_notes: Earliest CNY cut-off among UK banks (06:30). Up to 4 working days for non-EEA. No express option marketed separately.
providers.regulatory_status: Authorised by PRA, regulated by FCA and PRA (FRN: 765112)
providers.years_operating_note: HSBC UK since 1992 (acquired Midland Bank). Midland Bank founded 1836. Group founded 1865 in Hong Kong.
providers.max_transfer: GBP 50,000/day online; GBP 10,000/day phone (standard); up to GBP 10,000,000/day (Premier phone)
providers.forward_contracts_note: Available for business/corporate customers. Likely NDF for CNY.

providers.fx_markup_notes: Markup tiered by assets held with Barclays ('FX price plan'). Card foreign transaction fee: 2.75% (business debit), 2.99% (business credit). Barclays does not publish the exchange rate markup percentage.
providers.transfer_fee_notes: Free online. GBP 25 by phone/branch. GBP 6 optional Overseas Delivery Charge to cover intermediary fees. GBP 3 USD cover charge. Receiving: GBP 6 for non-SEPA over GBP 100.
providers.intermediary_notes: SHA default online. OUR available via Overseas Delivery Charge (GBP 6). CNAPS 12-digit code required for China transfers. Variable intermediary deductions under SHA.
providers.account_cost_notes: Personal account free. International Banking Account free if GBP 100k+ average balance, otherwise GBP 40/month.
providers.speed_notes: Up to 8 working days per Barclays' own documentation. Typically 2-5 days. China-specific: 1-2 days reported in some cases.
providers.regulatory_status: Authorised by PRA, regulated by FCA and PRA (FRN: 759676 / 122702)
providers.years_operating_note: Oldest lineage among UK clearers, traces to 1690.

fx_markup_source.note: Wise blog: 3.55% on <10k, scaling to 1.5% on >250k

providers.fx_markup_notes: Heavily tiered by amount. Individual: 3.55% under GBP 10k, scaling to 1.5% over GBP 250k. Business: 2.6% under GBP 10k, scaling to 0.85% over GBP 1M. Best business rate of 0.85% requires GBP 1M+ conversion.
providers.transfer_fee_notes: GBP 9.50 personal. GBP 15 business. Plus GBP 12 correspondent fee (US/Canada/Europe) or GBP 20 (rest of world including China). Free between Lloyds accounts only.
providers.intermediary_notes: GBP 20 Lloyds-side correspondent charge for China (if OUR selected). SHA default online. Additional SWIFT chain deductions possible.
providers.account_cost_notes: Classic Account free. Club Lloyds GBP 3/month (waived if GBP 2,000+ paid in monthly). Business basic GBP 6.50/month.
providers.speed_notes: 2-5 working days via SWIFT. Same-day processing if before cut-off. No express option.
providers.regulatory_status: Authorised by PRA, regulated by FCA and PRA (FRN: 119278)
providers.supported_currencies: 61 currencies (business trade services); standard limited to GBP/EUR/USD
providers.forward_contracts_note: Corporate/commercial only. Not available to individuals.

fx_markup_source.note: Rutland FX: 3-5%, 'may charge significantly more than 2.5%'

providers.fx_markup_notes: Widest variance among UK banks. NatWest Markets publishes a 2.5% cap for business but actual personal rates measured at 4.6% by KeyCurrency. Exotic currencies like CNY likely at higher end. Impact on GBP 200k: GBP 6,000-8,000 in FX costs.
providers.transfer_fee_notes: Free online (standard). GBP 15 urgent online. Phone/branch: 0.3% of amount (min GBP 23, max GBP 40). GBP 10 agent charge for non-EEA. Receiving: GBP 7 for non-EUR over GBP 100.
providers.intermediary_notes: GBP 10 'agent charge' for non-EEA. SHA default. Additional SWIFT intermediary deductions variable and not disclosed in advance.
providers.account_cost_notes: Personal free. Business free for first 2 years, then transaction-based pricing.
providers.speed_notes: Standard 2-5 working days. Urgent option 1-3 days for GBP 15. Cut-off time for CNY not published.
providers.regulatory_status: Authorised by PRA, regulated by FCA and PRA (FRN: 121878)
providers.years_operating_note: Founded 1968 (merger). Constituent banks trace to 1650s (Royal Bank of Scotland).
fraud_protection_features: Daily transfer caps for scam prevention
providers.max_transfer: GBP 10,000/day online (biometric); GBP 750/day mobile (new payees, no biometric)
providers.forward_contracts_note: Available via NatWest Markets for business/corporate
providers.api_note: Bankline for business; NatWest Payit API for domestic
providers.limitations_notes: Lowest personal online limit among UK banks (GBP 10,000/day). Only 30+ currencies vs 50+ at peers.

providers.fx_markup_notes: Only UK bank to publish transparent tiered markup: up to GBP 10k = 3%, GBP 10-50k = 2%, GBP 50k+ = 1%. Most competitive at high amounts. Rates only visible to logged-in customers. Branch FX (physical currency) 4-7%.
providers.transfer_fee_notes: GBP 25 standard. FREE during promotional period 2 Feb - 28 Apr 2026 for non-GBP transfers from personal current accounts. SEPA free. Receiving: GBP 6-8. Edge account: 0% FX fee on debit card abroad.
providers.intermediary_notes: SHA default. OUR available. Recipients may receive GBP 20-50 less than sent under SHA.
providers.account_cost_notes: Everyday Account free. 1/2/3 Account GBP 4-5/month. Edge GBP 3/month. International Current Account available in GBP/EUR/USD.
providers.speed_notes: 3-5 working days for major currencies. 7-10 working days for emerging/less common currencies (may include CNY). Up to 4 working days per Santander's own documentation for non-EUR/USD. Slowest among the 7 banks for exotic currencies.
providers.regulatory_status: Authorised by PRA, regulated by FCA and PRA (FRN: 106054)
providers.years_operating_note: Since 2004 in UK (acquired Abbey National, itself founded 1944). Parent Banco Santander founded 1857.

total_cost_10k_gbp.calculation_method: fx_markup (2.5% for 10k tier = 2% per published schedule, using 2.5% typical as conservative) x 10000 + wire_fee (25) + intermediary_typical (15). Note: during Feb-Apr 2026 promo, wire fee would be 0.

providers.fx_markup_notes: Most moderate of the high-street banks. Nationwide does not publish the explicit markup percentage. Smaller amounts may see up to 4%.
providers.transfer_fee_notes: GBP 20 per SWIFT payment. GBP 0 for SEPA/euro to EEA. Waived for FlexOne, FlexStudent, FlexGraduate accounts.
providers.intermediary_notes: Uses HSBC as intermediary bank (MIDLGB22). Correspondent fees unpredictable and deducted from payment amount.
providers.account_cost_notes: FlexAccount free. FlexPlus GBP 13/month (includes travel insurance). Must hold a Nationwide current account.
providers.speed_notes: Non-EEA: 3-5 working days. EEA within 1 day. No express option. International transfers NOT available via mobile app — online banking or branch only.
providers.regulatory_status: Dual-regulated by FCA and PRA (FRN: 106078). Incorporated under Building Societies Act 1986.
providers.years_operating_note: Founded 1884. World's largest building society. ~18 million UK customers, ~GBP 170 billion deposits. Acquired Virgin Money (completed April 2026).
providers.limitations_notes: No forward contracts. No mobile app for international transfers. No API or batch payments. Trustpilot: 2/5.

fx_markup_source.note: TSB FAQ confirms 'FX International Payment Margin'

providers.fx_markup_notes: TSB's own FAQ confirms the margin exists. Third-party sources report 2.5-3.65% depending on currency and time of day. Non-sterling card transaction fee 2.99%.
providers.transfer_fee_notes: Online: GBP 10 (up to GBP 5,000) or GBP 17.50 (over GBP 5,000). Phone/branch: GBP 20 (non-EEA). Plus GBP 6 correspondent bank fee for non-EEA/Switzerland/Monaco. EUR to EEA free.
providers.intermediary_notes: GBP 6 correspondent fee charged by TSB for non-EEA. Additional SWIFT chain intermediaries may deduct further. Receiving: GBP 7 for non-euro over GBP 100.
providers.account_cost_notes: Current accounts free. Must hold TSB current account to send international payments.
providers.speed_notes: China falls under 'other currencies to EU/EEA/North America/Australia/NZ/Middle East/Far East: up to 4 working days'. All other destinations: more than 4 working days. Major routes ~4 days; other routes up to 12 days. Cut-off 4:30pm for same-day processing.
providers.regulatory_status: Authorised by PRA, regulated by FCA and PRA (FRN: 191240)
providers.parent_company: Banco de Sabadell S.A. (Spain). Note: Santander acquisition announced for GBP 2.65 billion, expected Q1 2026.
providers.years_operating_note: Spun out of Lloyds Banking Group 2014, sold to Sabadell 2015. Fined GBP 48.65M by FCA/PRA in 2022 for operational resilience failures.
providers.supported_currencies: 8 primary via mobile app; broader selection via online/phone/branch
providers.limitations_notes: GBP 75,000/day limit. Online banking hours for international: 7:30am-4:30pm weekdays. No forward contracts. Trustpilot: 4/5.

fx_markup_source.note: Wise uses mid-market rate with zero markup. ecbMarkupPercent = 0.0%.

providers.fx_markup_notes: Wise charges 0% FX markup — the mid-market rate is passed through exactly. However, all cost is embedded in the transparent percentage-based transfer fee (Dimension 2). For comparison purposes, Wise's effective total cost on GBP-to-CNY is 0.83-1.37% depending on amount and delivery method — economically equivalent to a 0.83-1.37% FX markup with zero fees. The distinction is presentation (fee shown upfront), not economics (same CNY arrives).

effective_total_cost_pct.note: Wise's fee expressed as an equivalent FX markup for like-for-like comparison

transfer_fee_components.note: Fixed + variable. Both vary by delivery method. Figures shown are for Alipay (most common CNY route).

transfer_fee_source.note: Wise GBP-CNY pricing page with live quotes

providers.transfer_fee_notes: Dual-fee model: fixed (GBP 2.45 for Alipay) + variable (0.89% for Alipay). Fee decreases at higher amounts due to flat fee dilution and volume discount tiers (kick in at GBP 20k+/month). Delivery method matters enormously: holding in Wise Balance costs 0.36% vs 0.91% via Alipay vs 1.88% via card. Credit card funding adds ~2.2% on top. Business and personal pricing is identical.
providers.intermediary_notes: No SWIFT, no intermediary fees. Delivers to China via local payment networks: Alipay, WeChat (Weixin), UnionPay card, and local bank transfer (B2B only). Recipient pays nothing.
providers.account_cost_notes: Free account. No setup, monthly, or minimum balance. Multi-currency account holds 50+ currencies for free.
providers.speed_notes: 74% of transfers arrive in under 20 seconds (instant). 95% within 1 day. Alipay: same day to ~9 hours. WeChat: recipient must claim within 48 hours. Bank transfer (B2B): 1-2 working days. Alipay recipients must complete verification within 72 hours.
providers.regulatory_status: Authorised as Electronic Money Institution by FCA (FRN: 900507). Licensed under EMR 2011 and PSR 2017.
providers.fund_safeguarding_note: Customer funds safeguarded via deposits at regulated banking institutions and investment in high-quality liquid assets (government bonds). Funds kept separate from operating money. Not a bank — does not lend customer funds. NOT FSCS-covered.
providers.max_transfer: Per-transaction CNY limits: UnionPay 33,000 CNY; Alipay 50,000 CNY; WeChat 50,000 CNY. Bank transfer (B2B): no per-transaction limit.
providers.forward_contracts_note: Not available for personal. Wise Business may offer FX risk management.
providers.api_note: Wise Platform API for business integrations
providers.limitations_notes: B2B bank transfer restricted to certain banks. Without recipient National ID, limited to ICBC, Bank of Communications, and select provincial banks. Cannot send to China Merchants, Minsheng, Industrial Bank, Shanghai Pudong Development, Bank of Nanjing for personal transfers. Trustpilot: 4.3/5 (280,000+ reviews).

total_cost_10k_gbp.calculation_method: fx_markup (0%) + Wise fee on GBP 10k via Alipay (GBP 90.66 = 0.91%) + intermediary (0). Via WeChat would be GBP 66.23 (0.66%). Via Wise Balance would be GBP 35.87 (0.36%).

providers.fx_markup_notes: Variable 0.4-2.0%. Depends on currency pair, amount (larger = better), and timing (weekends/holidays = higher). GBP-CNY likely 1.0-2.0% due to less common pair. OFX does not publish exact margins. Revenue comes entirely from FX spread (no transfer fees).
providers.transfer_fee_notes: GBP 0. No transfer fees, no setup fees, no account fees.
providers.intermediary_notes: OFX uses SWIFT/correspondent banking. No intermediary fees charged by OFX, but recipient/intermediary banks may deduct fees. OFX does not control or disclose these.
providers.speed_notes: Most transfers 1-4 business days. China specifically 3-5 business days. UK Faster Payments clears within 2 hours for funding.
providers.regulatory_status: Authorised as Electronic Money Institution by FCA (FRN: 902028). Also ICO registered (Z9240083).
providers.fund_safeguarding_note: Client funds held in segregated accounts. Regulated across 8 jurisdictions: FCA (UK), ASIC (Australia), FINTRAC (Canada), FMA (NZ), MAS (Singapore), FinCEN (USA).
providers.years_operating_note: Founded 1998 (as OzForex in Australia). UK entity: UKForex Limited.
providers.max_transfer: No self-imposed maximum (verification status affects practical limits, often exceeds USD 500,000)
providers.forward_contracts_note: Lock in rate for 2 days to 12 months. Deposit typically 10% (personal) or 5% (business). Limit orders available.
providers.limitations_notes: CRITICAL: CNY transfers restricted to BUSINESS customers only. Personal users cannot send CNY to China via OFX. Trustpilot: 4.4/5 (11,000+ reviews).

total_cost_10k_gbp.calculation_method: fx_markup_typical (1.0%) x 10000 + wire_fee (0) + intermediary_typical (10). Note: OFX CNY is business-only.

fx_markup_source.note: 0.5% above interbank for major currencies including CNY

providers.fx_markup_notes: Direct WorldFirst competitor at identical price point (0.5% FX). Claims 'over 80% cheaper than UK high street banks'. Local CNY rails bypass SWIFT entirely. BUSINESS ONLY.
providers.transfer_fee_notes: Local CNY transfers: FREE. SWIFT transfers: GBP 10-20. Receiving from non-owner accounts: 0.3%. Account tiers: Explore free (or GBP 19/mo if <GBP 10k balance), Grow GBP 49/mo, Accelerate from GBP 999/mo.
providers.intermediary_notes: Local CNY rails: no SWIFT, no intermediary fees. SWIFT route also available but carries GBP 10-20 fee and intermediary risk.
providers.monthly_fee_note: Explore plan free if GBP 10k+ balance/deposits; otherwise GBP 19/mo
providers.speed_notes: Local CNY: same-day or next business day. SWIFT: 2-5 days. Local CNY declaration review: 0-2 business days additional.
providers.regulatory_status: FCA-authorised EMI (FRN: 900876). Regulated in multiple jurisdictions.
providers.api_note: Full API for business integrations, batch payments
providers.limitations_notes: BUSINESS ONLY — no personal transfers. Local CNY eligibility restricted: (1) businesses registered in mainland China with Chinese national legal rep/UBO, (2) foreign reps with passport + Chinese bank card, (3) e-commerce/retail sellers exporting from China. All local CNY transfers require declaration documentation. SWIFT universally available without restrictions.

total_cost_10k_gbp.calculation_method: fx_markup_typical (0.5%) x 10000 + local_transfer_fee (0) + intermediary (0). Note: business only, local CNY eligibility restrictions apply.

fx_markup_source.note: Markup embedded in rate, not separately disclosed

providers.fx_markup_notes: Markup embedded in exchange rate, not shown separately. Ranges from ~1% (large economy transfers) to 3.7% (small express transfers). First GBP 750 gets a promotional rate. Express delivery has worse rates than economy.
providers.transfer_fee_notes: GBP 1.49 flat fee. First transfer free. Does not vary by amount.
providers.intermediary_notes: Local delivery via Alipay, WeChat Pay, or UnionPay bank deposit. No SWIFT, no intermediary deductions.
providers.speed_notes: Express: minutes to hours (Alipay/WeChat). Economy: 3-5 business days (bank deposit). Express costs more in exchange rate markup.
providers.regulatory_status: FCA-authorised payment institution (FRN: 728639). HMRC registered MSB.
providers.limitations_notes: PERSONAL REMITTANCE ONLY — not designed for B2B supplier payments. Delivery to Alipay/WeChat requires recipient's registered account. Express delivery has worse FX rates than economy.

total_cost_10k_gbp.calculation_method: fx_markup_typical (2.0%) x 10000 + flat_fee (1.49) + intermediary (0). Note: personal only, actual markup varies by delivery speed.

fx_markup_source.note: XE acknowledges markup exists but does not publish %

providers.fx_markup_notes: XE shows mid-market rate on their converter but applies a markup on actual send rate. Not transparent — markup varies by amount (larger = lower), currency pair, and market conditions. XE deliberately does not publish exact percentages.
providers.transfer_fee_notes: Often GBP 0 for bank transfer funding. Card payments carry a small fee (amount not published). 'Some transfers to China come with a small fee.'
providers.intermediary_notes: Mix of local rails and SWIFT depending on amount/route. Bank deposits can arrive in minutes (suggesting local rails for some transfers). No specific intermediary fee disclosure.
providers.speed_notes: 1-4 working days standard. Many transfers same-day or instant once funds received.
providers.regulatory_status: FCA-regulated. Owned by Euronet Worldwide (NASDAQ: EEFT).
providers.max_transfer: $535,000 USD equivalent per online transfer. Contact team for larger.
providers.limitations_notes: Both personal and business. High limits ($535k). Forward contracts may be available. Pricing is opaque. Cash pickup available at 500k+ locations worldwide.

total_cost_10k_gbp.calculation_method: fx_markup_typical (1.0%) x 10000 + transfer_fee (0) + intermediary_estimated (5). Pricing opaque — actual cost may vary significantly.

fx_markup_source.note: Handbook: 'Up to 0.5%' FX ceiling for UK

providers.fx_markup_notes: Published ceiling of 0.5% for UK corridor. This is the maximum — actual rates may be lower. The research uses the 0.5% ceiling per the conservative approach agreed in the Research Plan. WorldFirst does not use a percentage-based transfer fee on top of FX.
providers.transfer_fee_notes: Local GBP: GBP 0.30/txn. International: GBP 4/txn. China payment: 0.40%. 1688 World Pay: not listed for UK.
providers.intermediary_notes: L2L (Local-to-Local) transfers: zero intermediary fees. WorldFirst uses direct payment rails to China, bypassing SWIFT correspondent banks. 96% of SME costs are hidden in FX rates according to WorldFirst's own research.
providers.account_cost_notes: World Account free to open. No monthly fees. No minimum balance. Multi-user, multi-account.
providers.speed_notes: 80% of payments arrive same day in CNY. Direct payment rails to China — no SWIFT delays.
providers.regulatory_status: FCA authorised. Operating entity for Singapore: Alipay Merchant Services Pte. Ltd.
providers.years_operating_note: Founded ~2004. Part of Ant International since 2019. UEFA EURO 2024 official payment partner.
providers.can_pay_1688_note: World Pay direct checkout on 1688.com and TaoWorld
providers.api_note: Xero and Netsuite integrations. API availability region-dependent.

total_cost_10k_gbp.calculation_method: fx_markup_max (0.5%) x 10000 + intl_wire_fee (4) + intermediary (0). Conservative: uses maximum published FX ceiling.

savings_analysis.note: Estimated savings based on typical bank FX markup rates for this corridor. WorldFirst costs use the maximum published FX markup ceiling of 0.5%. Actual bank costs vary by account type, relationship, transfer amount, and market conditions.

comparison_table.calculation_notes: Bank: avg 3.1% FX markup x 5000 + avg GBP 22 fee + GBP 15 intermediary. WF: 0.5% x 5000 + GBP 4.
Bank: avg 3.1% FX markup x 10000 + avg GBP 22 fee + GBP 15 intermediary. WF: 0.5% x 10000 + GBP 4.
Bank: avg 2.8% FX markup x 25000 (lower rate on larger amounts) + avg GBP 22 fee + GBP 15 intermediary. WF: 0.5% x 25000 + GBP 4.
Bank: avg 2.5% FX markup x 50000 (volume discount) + avg GBP 22 fee + GBP 15 intermediary. WF: 0.5% x 50000 + GBP 4.

data_quality_assessment.overall: HIGH — GB-CNY is the best-documented corridor. Seven UK banks researched across all seven dimensions with strong third-party sourcing.
strong_points: FX markup data for all 7 banks cross-referenced across 3-5 independent sources each
Transfer fees published by most banks on their own websites
FCA registration numbers and regulatory status verified via FCA Register
Wise 2024 transparency study provides standardised markup comparison across all major UK banks
Santander uniquely publishes tiered FX markup on their website — strongest single bank data point
WorldFirst data sourced from published product handbook with corridor-specific pricing
gaps: No UK bank publishes their GBP-CNY specific markup — all FX data is for major pairs (EUR, USD). CNY markups may be higher due to exotic currency premium.
Intermediary fee estimates are ranges, not precise — SWIFT chain deductions are inherently unpredictable
Transfer speed to China is estimated for most banks — none publish China-specific delivery SLAs
Lloyds and Nationwide transfer fees sourced from third parties, not directly from bank websites
OFX FX markup is a range (0.4-2.0%) — they do not publish exact rates per currency pair

compliance_notes.methodology: Cost data collected from provider websites, independent comparison sites (Wise, Monito, Exiap, KeyCurrency, Rutland FX, MoneyTransferComparison), and financial publications (Finextra). FX markup calculated as the percentage difference between provider's offered rate and the mid-market (interbank) rate sourced from XE.com and Bank of England. All comparisons standardised to a GBP 10,000 bank-to-bank transfer from the UK to China.
assumptions: Transfer amount: GBP 10,000 (unless otherwise stated in savings analysis)
Account type: Standard personal or SME online banking (not Premier/Private/Corporate)
Payment method: Online bank transfer (not branch, phone, or card)
Pricing: standard rates without negotiation or relationship discounts
WorldFirst FX markup: maximum published ceiling of 0.5% (actual rates may be lower)
compliance_notes.disclaimer: Cost estimates based on published rates and independent comparison data as of 2 April 2026. Actual bank costs vary by account type, relationship, transfer amount, and market conditions. WorldFirst costs shown use the maximum published FX markup of 0.5% for the UK corridor; actual rates may be lower. This comparison is for illustrative purposes and does not constitute financial advice.
